# Climate Change Impact

Rising global temperatures are causing more frequent extreme weather events. These changes affect agriculture, water supply, and human health across multiple regions.

## Economic Consequences

The agricultural sector faces declining yields in tropical regions. Smallholder farmers are disproportionately affected by droughts and floods.

| Region | Impact Level | Primary Risk |
|--------|-------------|--------------|
| Sub-Saharan Africa | High | Drought |
| Southeast Asia | Medium | Flooding |
| Northern Europe | Low | Temperature shift |

- Carbon emissions continue to rise despite international agreements
- Renewable energy adoption is accelerating but remains insufficient
- Policy interventions require coordinated global action
